content.cn_text: 学生F =女学生;学生M =学生男
content.en_text: Student F = Student Female; Student M = Student Male

content.cn_text: 讲师:谢谢大家今天来听关于恐鸟的故事，这种动物已经灭绝很久了。首先，我们来看看恐鸟是什么。恐鸟是新西兰特有的九种不会飞的鸟类。数千年来，它们一直是新西兰森林、灌木丛和亚高山生态系统中的主要食草动物，但大约500年前，它们都灭绝了。当我提到古代灭绝的动物时，你可能会立即想到恐龙，它们在6600万年前消失了。我们用来研究的恐龙化石数量很多，但恐鸟的化石保存得不多，尽管这两种动物今天对人们都很有吸引力。
content.en_text: Lecturer: Thank you all for coming here today to hear about the Moa, a kind of animal which has been extinct for a long time. Well, first of all we'll look at what the Moa are. The Moa are nine species of flightless birds endemic to New Zealand. They were the dominant herbivores in New Zealand's forest, shrubland and subalpine ecosystems for thousands of years, but around 500 years ago, they all went extinct. When I mention extinct animals during ancient times, you may immediately think of dinosaurs,which disappeared around 66 million years ago. Fossils of dinosaurs which we use to study are large in number, but not many of those of Moa remain, though both animals appeal to people today.

content.cn_text: 学生F:所以恐鸟现在听起来更神秘了。但是先生，我对这些不会飞的鸟有个问题。我们如何将它们与其他鸟类区分开来?
content.en_text: Student F: So the Moa sound more mysterious now. But sir, I've got a question about these flightless birds. How can we distinguish them from other birds?

content.cn_text: 这是个好问题!鸟类通常的特征是温血动物，有羽毛和翅膀，通常能够飞行和产卵，而不会飞的恐鸟。在灭绝之前，它们是世界上最大的鸟类。它们的头相对于它们的身体来说相对较小，它们是它们家族中唯一没有翅膀的鸟类，甚至没有退化的翅膀和结实的尾骨。
content.en_text: Lecturer: That's a good question! Birds are commonly characterised by being warm-blooded,having feathers and wings usually capable of flight, and laying eggs, while the flightless Moa. until their extinction, were the largest birds in the world. Their heads are relatively small in relation to their bodies, and they are the only wingless birds lacking even the vestigial wings and substantial tail bones in their family.

content.cn_text: 学生
content.en_text: Student

content.cn_text: 这是令人印象深刻。但他们天生就是这样吗?我是说当她们还是小女孩的时候。M:
content.en_text: That's impressive. But were they bom to be like that? I mean when they were chicks.M:

content.cn_text: 是的，当然!让我们来看看小鸡。恐鸟的蛋在巢中孵化约两个月。雏鸟在孵化后发育良好，可能很快就能离开巢穴自己觅食。
content.en_text: Lecturer: Yes, absolutely! So let's move onto the chicks. The eggs of Moa were laid in nests and incubated for about two months. The chicks would be well-developed upon hatching and probably would be able to leave the nests to feed on their own almost immediately.

content.cn_text: 学生F:我听说是雄性恐鸟孵的蛋。这是真的吗?
content.en_text: Student F: I've heard that the male Moa are thought to have incubated the eggs. Is that true?

content.cn_text: 我认为这是有可能的。我在某个地方读到，从蛋壳外表面提取的性别特异性DNA表明，这些蛋很可能是由雄性孵化的，但我们仍需要更多的咨询。
content.en_text: Lecturer: I think there is a possibility for that. I've read somewhere that the sex-specific DNA recovered from the outer surfaces of eggshells suggested that these eggs were likely to have been hatched by the male, but we still need to consult more.

content.cn_text: 但我有一个问题。偶尔也有人猜测恐鸟还活着，因为有人说他们在19世纪末甚至20世纪在新西兰看到过它们。你觉得有可能吗?
content.en_text: But I have a question. There has been some occasional speculation that the Moa were still alive, because someone said they had caught sight of them in New Zealand in the late 19th century or even the 20th. Do you think it's possible?

content.cn_text: 学生:我并不感到惊讶，因为这种说法已经有好几次了。M:但是我觉得很有趣，因为没有发现恐鸟足迹的可靠证据，专家们仍然认为恐鸟生存的可能性极小。
content.en_text: Student I'm not amazed by that, since that kind of thing has been claimed several times,M: but I find it funny because no reliable evidence of Moa tracks has ever been found,and experts still contend that Moa survival is extremely unlikely.

content.cn_text: 学生F:那么恐鸟灭绝的原因是什么呢?我想知道是全球变暖还是其他一些与他们的生活环境有关的因素。
content.en_text: Student F: So what was the reason for the Moa's extinction? I wonder if it was global warming or some other factors related to their living environment.

content.cn_text: 讲师:嗯，在人类定居者到达新西兰之前
content.en_text: Lecturer: Well, before the arrival of human settlers in New Zealand, the

content.cn_text: 恐鸟唯一的捕食者是巨大的哈斯鹰。然后，毛利人在公元1300年之前的某个时候来到了这里，所有的恐鸟都很快被狩猎灭绝了。多么可怕的事情!
content.en_text: Moa's only predator was the massive Haas's eagle. Then the Maori arrived sometime before CE 1300, and all Moa genera were soon driven to extinction by hunting. What a horrible thing!

content.cn_text: 好的。现在让我们来看看一些恐鸟的特征。南岛
content.en_text: Lecturer: All right. Now let's look at the features of some species of Moa. The South Island

content.cn_text: 巨型恐鸟可能是已知的最高的鸟类，在9种恐鸟中第二高的是北岛巨型恐鸟，雌性的体重和身高都明显大于雄性。
content.en_text: Giant Moa may have been the tallest birds ever known, and the second tallest of the nine moa species is the North Island Giant Moa, with the females being markedly larger than males both in weight and height.

content.cn_text: 学生
content.en_text: Student

content.cn_text: 我听说最小的恐鸟是海岸恐鸟。对吗?M:
content.en_text: And I've heard that the smallest of the Moa birds are the Coastal Moa. Is that right?M:

content.cn_text: 是的，你说得对。你以前听说过其他恐鸟吗?
content.en_text: Lecturer: Yes, you're right. And have you heard about any other kinds of Moa before?

content.cn_text: 学生F:我知道冠毛恐鸟。它们下的蛋可能比其他的蛋大。
content.en_text: Student F: I know the Crested Moa. The eggs they laid may be larger than others'.

content.cn_text: 讲师:由于它们主要生活在南岛偏远的内陆地区，它们的化石在考古遗址中很少见或没有发现，也没有发现蛋的遗骸。
content.en_text: Lecturer: As they mainly lived in the remote interior of the Southern Island, their fossils are rare or absent in archaeological sites, and no egg remains have yet been identified.

content.cn_text: 学生F:有没有哪种恐鸟的化石更多?
content.en_text: Student F: Are there any species of Moa that have got more fossils?

content.cn_text: 是的，当然。由于它们的栖息地保存完好，因此有相当数量的粗足恐鸟的遗迹存在。它们的头骨显示出相对较差的视力，良好的嗅觉和非常短的喙。
content.en_text: Lecturer: Yes, of course. A considerable amount of remains of the Stoutlegged Moa exist,due to the well-preserved properties of their habitat. Their skulls reveal relatively bad vision, a good sense of smell, and a very short bill.

content.cn_text: 然后是东部恐鸟。它们的气管又长又窄，这可能使它们能发出比其他恐鸟更响亮、更有共鸣的叫声，它们的发声能力也最强，所以它们在森林里或晚上看不见彼此时也能交流。在寻找食物时，除了声音，它们还使用了一系列的感官，比如嗅觉和视觉。
content.en_text: Then there is the Eastern Moa. They were remarkable in having very long and narrow windpipes, which probably enabled them to make louder, more resonant calls than those of other Moa, and have the greatest vocal abilities, so they could communicate when they could not see each other in the forest or at night. They used a range of senses, apart from sound in their search for food, such as their sense of smell and vision.

groups.desc:
Questions 21-26
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: What is the thing that makes the Moa similar to dinosaur?

options.title: A
options.content: Both are of interest to the public.

options.title: B
options.content: Both are extinct at similar time.

options.title: C
options.content: Both left lots of fossil remains

items.analysis: 本题的正确答案是A选项。原文中提到恐龙和莫阿鸟的化石数量有所不同，但最后强调"both animals appeal to people today"（这两种动物如今都吸引着人们的兴趣）。这直接对应了A选项"Both are of interest to the public"（两者都引起公众兴趣）。文中虽然提到了化石数量和灭绝时间的差异，但唯一的相似点是它们都受到现代人的关注。

items.title: What is the difference between Moa and other birds?
items.analysis: 文中明确指出恐鸟是唯一没有翅膀的鸟类，甚至连退化的翅膀都没有。这是恐鸟与其他鸟类最显著的区别，因为大多数鸟类都有翅膀，即使是不能飞行的鸟类通常也有退化的翅膀。因此，选项A"no wing bones"（没有翅膀骨头）是正确答案，它最准确地描述了恐鸟与其他鸟类的主要区别。

items.title: What's the special feature of their chicks?

options.title: A
options.content: They never return to the nests.

options.title: B
options.content: Most of them die within two months after birth.

options.title: C
options.content: They can find food by themselves.

items.analysis: 文中明确指出小鸟在孵化后就发育良好（well-developed upon hatching），并且能够几乎立即离开巢穴自己觅食（leave the nests to feed on their own almost immediately）。这直接对应了选项C"They can find food by themselves"（它们能自己寻找食物）。这个描述强调了小鸟的独立性和自给自足的能力，与题目询问的"特殊特征"相符。

items.title: What is the tutor's opinion on male hatching the eggs?

options.title: A
options.content: He doubts whether it is true or possible.

options.title: B
options.content: He thinks it may be true.

options.title: C
options.content: He can say with certainty that it is true.

items.analysis: 讲师的回答表明他认为雄性孵蛋的说法可能是真的，但并不完全确定。他首先说"I think there is a possibility for that"，直接表示了这种可能性。然后他提到了支持这种说法的证据（DNA分析），但同时也指出"we still need to consult more"，表示还需要更多研究。这种表述方式明确表示了一种可能性，而不是完全的怀疑或绝对的确定。因此，B选项"He thinks it may be true"最准确地反映了讲师的观点。

items.title: What is the male student's response after hearing some people see a Moa recently
items.analysis: 男学生的回答表明他对有人声称看到莫阿鸟的说法感到好笑。他使用了"funny"这个词来描述他的感受，这直接对应了选项C "amused"。他提到这种说法已经多次出现，表明他对此习以为常，而不是惊讶。他还指出没有可靠的证据支持这种说法，这进一步强调了他觉得这种说法可笑的态度。

items.title: Why did the Moa become extinct?
items.analysis: 文中明确指出恐鸟是因为人类的狩猎活动而灭绝的。毛利人的到来和随之而来的狩猎行为直接导致了恐鸟的灭绝，这明显属于人类干预（human interference）的范畴。因此，B选项"human interference"是正确答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to
Questions 27-30.

items.title: the North Island Giant Moa
items.analysis: 文中明确指出北岛巨型恐鸟的雌性在体重和身高上都明显大于雄性。这直接对应了选项A "the much taller female"（体型更高大的雌性）。这是对北岛巨型恐鸟最显著的描述，因此A是最符合题意的答案。

items.title: the Crested Moa
items.analysis: 文中明确提到Crested Moa的化石在考古遗址中很少见或根本不存在（"their fossils are rare or absent in archaeological sites"），这直接对应了选项B "less fossils left"（留下的化石较少）。此外，原文还补充说没有发现任何蛋的遗迹，进一步强调了Crested Moa遗留下来的化石和遗迹非常稀少。因此，B选项是最符合原文描述的正确答案。

items.title: the Stout-legged Moa
items.analysis: 文中明确提到Stoutlegged Moa的头骨显示出"relatively bad vision"（相对较差的视力），这与选项F "poor eyesight"（视力差）直接对应。"bad vision"和"poor eyesight"在这个语境下是同义表达，因此F是正确答案。

items.title: the Eastern Moa
items.analysis: 文中明确指出Eastern Moa具有很长而窄的气管，这使它们能够发出比其他Moa更响亮、更有共鸣的叫声，并且拥有最强的发声能力。这些特征直接对应了选项E "better vocal sound"（更好的发声能力）。因此，E是正确答案。
